repo_prior_art.cite: doc/API.md §3.1 Manski bounded-outcome entries only; no heavy-tail MSM CATE entry

published_prior_art.cite: YadlowskyNamkoongBasuDuchiTian2022 gives sharp CATE/ATE bounds under bounded odds-ratio hidden confounding; Theorem 1's primal program is largely the same variational endpoint form with a moment-envelope relabel

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_qte_marginal_sensitivity/v1 targets CQTE certificates, not CATE mean tail-budget trimming

published_prior_art.cite: KallusMaoZhou2019CATEIntervals gives functional CATE intervals under hidden confounding; no verified alpha-Pareto tail-arm formula
Manski1990 and AronowLee2013 are support/probability bound comparators, not alpha-moment MSM width factorisations
AsiaeePalBeckHuling2026SharpGeneralization flags unbounded LR vacuity; JungKang2026ITBounds gives f-divergence conditional bounds without isolating alpha*=1
OprescuDornGhoummaidJessonKallusShalit2023 B-Learner already gives efficient sharp CATE bounds under hidden confounding; heavy-tail alpha-stable rate remains a narrower extension

structure_flags.label: Bibliography
structure_flags.one_line: §10 names Rolski-Schmidli-Schmidt-Teugels as a classical tail reference but §13 has no corresponding bibliography entry.

structure_flags.label: Bibliography
structure_flags.one_line: DornGuoKallus2025 is listed as JASA forthcoming, but the verified publication is JASA 120(549):331-342, 2025; the bib entry is stale.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1 / Conjecture 5
novelty_flags.one_line: The checklist misses Oprescu et al. 2023 B-Learner, which is a closer CATE-level hidden-confounding comparator than DornGuoKallus2025 alone.

novelty_flags.label: YadlowskyNamkoongBasuDuchiTian2022 comparison
novelty_flags.one_line: §4/§5 overstate the gap by describing Yadlowsky et al. as a sub-Gaussian/sub-exponential-envelope result; the verified paper states sharp CATE bounds for real-valued outcomes under bounded odds-ratio hidden confounding and root-n ATE inference under nuisance-rate conditions.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2 / Conjecture 4
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship comparisons to KallusMaoZhou2019CATEIntervals and JungKang2026ITBounds are cited without section/equation/page anchors tying them to the precise quantile-trimming and critical-tail-index gaps.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 4
soundness_flags.one_line: The claimed alpha*=1 vacuity is mathematically incompatible with R2 as written: at alpha=1, E|Y|<=sigma gives finite first moments, and bounded MSM likelihood-ratio tilts preserve finite means, so W_alpha cannot be +infty on an open class.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2 / Exhibit 9.1
soundness_flags.one_line: The worked Pareto witness is invalid: a unit Pareto with shape 1.5 has infinite 1.5th moment, and with sigma=1 and q≈1.929 there is no probability law on [q,infty) satisfying E[Y^1.5]<=1.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The advertised closed form for T_alpha uses an asymptotic O(q^{-(alpha-1)}) term, y^alpha instead of |y|^alpha, and is undefined for negative or too-large q relative to the moment budget.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Theorem 1 essentially restates the definition of the upper endpoint as the supremum over the same admissible Q set; the proof sketch adds compactness/attainment but no new sharp endpoint characterization.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 4 / Exhibit 9.2
soundness_flags.one_line: The claimed weak*-open divergent class is not well-posed: moment-bounded sets are not weak*-open, and the cited TV-open perturbation does not establish weak*-openness inside the alpha=1 moment envelope.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the false alpha*=1 phase transition with the mathematically coherent frontier: finite width for alpha>=1 and possible vacuity only for alpha<1, then restate every width and rate claim around that boundary.

Rebuild Exhibit 9.1 using a distribution whose alpha-th absolute moment is actually finite and whose support/quantile/moment budget are mutually feasible; compute the extremal object from primitives without asymptotic big-O terms.
Add Oprescu et al. 2023 B-Learner as a main CATE-level comparator and specify exactly what the heavy-tail alpha-rate result contributes beyond existing sharp CATE bounds under hidden confounding.

If retaining a flagship target, anchor each comparator gap to a precise section/equation/page and state the main result as a strict extension over a verified published theorem rather than as a generic heavy-tail relabelling.

recommended_next_step: Reject this version and redraft around a corrected tail-moment frontier before spending Stage 0 budget.
